vocab.meaning: ngăn chặn, át đi âm thanh
vocab.example: I often wear noise-cancelling headphones to block out the constant hum of the air conditioner.

vocab.meaning: vang lên một cách rõ rệt, đanh thép
vocab.example: The sound of construction work rings out across the neighborhood starting from early morning.

vocab.meaning: lấn át, làm cho không nghe thấy âm thanh khác
vocab.example: The roar of the jet engines usually drowns out any conversation on the street near the airport.

vocab.meaning: bùng phát, đột ngột trở nên ồn ào
vocab.example: Traffic noise tends to flare up during rush hour, making it difficult to concentrate on my work.

vocab.meaning: trôi dạt vào, vô tình nghe thấy (âm thanh từ xa)
vocab.example: The distant sound of a radio often drifts in through my open window in the late afternoon.

vocab.meaning: bùng nổ, vang lên bất thình lình
vocab.example: Laughter often bursts out from the playground nearby, which is a quite pleasant sound to hear.

vocab.meaning: nhạt dần, nhỏ dần rồi biến mất
vocab.example: I listen to the sirens of ambulances as they slowly fade away into the distance.

vocab.meaning: xé tan, xuyên qua (màn đêm/không gian yên tĩnh)
vocab.example: The sharp honking of a car horn occasionally cuts through the silence of the night.

vocab.meaning: phát ra âm thanh trầm, vang dội
vocab.example: Announcements from the local loudspeaker boom out every morning to inform residents of the news.

vocab.meaning: vang xa, truyền đi một khoảng cách dài
vocab.example: Because the walls are quite thin, the noise from my neighbor's TV often carries over into my bedroom.
